rows.notes: Top-line description and numbers are clear and well aligned to the three ruling lines on the right. Pounds (280), shillings (0) and pence (1) are unambiguous.
Description legible as 'Gas Lights' with date; numeric columns are clear (27 5 10) and validate correctly.
Abbreviated 'Do' (ditto) and 'Mich' visible. Amount 8/11/0 aligned beneath previous amounts; handwriting of description a bit faint, but numbers are clear and within valid ranges.

rows.description: Mr. Falconer 19th Feb. on 24? 14? 2? 3? Costs to Feby 1850
rows.notes: Surname 'Falconer' legible; the rest of the descriptive text contains cramped numerals and abbreviations that are hard to decipher (marked with '?'). The monetary figures (6/8/6) are reasonably clear and align with the pounds/shillings/pence columns, but the description text is uncertain.

Text reads 'Subscriptions Viz. Infirmary'. There is an amount printed on the same line (1/1/0); this could be a header line in other contexts, but here the amount is present and aligned, so it has been classified as an entry. Description is legible; amount alignment is clear.
Place name 'Grey Court School' clear and amount 20/0/0 sharply written and aligned in the pounds/shillings/pence columns.
Second item in the subscriptions list reads as 'Wellington Do' (Do = ditto). Amount 5/5/0 is legible and aligns well.
Description 'South Kensington Do' readable; amount 5/5/0 clear and in valid range.

The place name is somewhat ambiguous in the handwriting (interpreted as 'Little Town Do'); the numeric amount 6/0/0 is clear and well aligned. Description reading could plausibly be another similar name.
Handwritten place name reads as 'Garston Do' (legible enough); amount 3/3/0 is clear and properly aligned.
'Morton Do' is legible in the list. Amount 5/5/0 is clear and aligns with the shilling column.
Entry reads 'Garrington Do' (script clear enough). Amount 3/3/0 aligns with the column guides and is unambiguous.
Abbreviated placename beginning with 'L.' followed by 'Wittenham Do' (handwriting moderately clear). Amount 5/5/0 is clear.
Reads as 'Bensington Do'; amount 2/2/0 is distinct and positioned correctly under the pounds/shillings/pence guides.
Place name is a bit unclear (interpreted as 'Gunkar Do'); amount 5/0/0 is clear. Description could be another similar parish name.
Text reads as 'Poor of Grimcar' (poor relief entry). Amount 10/0/0 is bold and clear in the pounds column.
Formal body name 'Diocesan Board of Education' is legible; amount 10/0/0 clearly written and aligned.

rows.description: Small Lumps in Diocese of Lon
rows.notes: Description is difficult to read; interpreted as something like 'Small Lump(s) in Diocese of ...'. Amount 5/0/0 is clear, but the exact wording of the description is uncertain due to faded/looped letters.
Reads 'Widows & Orphans of Clergy Do'. The amount 5/0/0 is clearly aligned; description is fairly legible.
Text appears as 'University Marshalle' (final letters slightly flourishy). Amount 2/0/0 is small but clear. Slight uncertainty in exact spelling of the office title.
Reads 'Bele Ringer & Chapel Clerk' (interpreted as 'Bell Ringer' but original spells 'Bele'); amount 10/0/0 is clear and bold.
Short description 'Corn Prices' clear. Amount 1/19/6 is legible and validates (shillings < 20, pence < 12).
'Easter Offerings' is readable; amount 1/1/0 clearly aligned and unambiguous.
Entry 'Parish Rates' is legible. There is additional marginal scribble referencing pro-rata / church rate, but the main amount 21/13/4 is clearly written and aligns with the columns.
Description 'Synodals and Procurations' legible; amount 2/16/8 is clear and valid. Slight ink thinning but reading is unambiguous.
Label indicates 'College Gardener - 1st Quarter'. Amount 7/12/0 is well aligned and legible.
Second-quarter entry abbreviated as '2nd Do'. Amount 7/13/9 is visible though the pence digit has slight flourish; still within valid pence range.
Third-quarter line '3rd Do' with amount 12/15/11. Pence '11' is clear though the ink is slightly heavier; still unambiguous.
Fourth-quarter '4th Do' visible; amount 7/12/0 aligned neatly under the columns.
Description is slightly uncertain due to script (interpreted as 'Interest on Church Deposit' or similar). Amount 6/0/0 is clear. Chosen wording reflects the most likely reading.
Heading 'Open Scholarships' followed by an individual name (read as 'Mr Hemper'). The amount 40/0/0 is large and clearly written.
Name is faint/looped; interpreted as 'Mr Wilzeefs' or similar. The amount 40/0/0 is clear and consistent with the two sibling scholarship lines.
Final scholarship name is uncertain (read as 'Mr Roase' / 'Mr Hoase' / 'Mr Gouse'); handwriting of the name is indistinct, but the numeric amount 40/0/0 is clear and aligned.
Bottom-right grand total denoted by the pound symbol 'L' and boxed. Numerals 619/3/7 are large and clear; alignment confirms column boundaries. High confidence.
